mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, severe labor market slack suppresses corporate earnings growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the t-statistic for the return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer demand recovery, which forces equity prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, supply-side bottlenecks and falling capacity utilization trigger short-term equity sell-offs, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines coincide with positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP reflect expanding aggregate demand that translates into higher corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters yield negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 218.0, inflation expectations accelerate and force markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 218.0 are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict positive returns in more than 55% of months, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, rising industrial production signals capacity utilization improvements that precede earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases yield negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the predictive signal disappears when unemployment exceeds 8.5%.
